title: Bubble Bliss POS — API Integration Guide
overview: This guide covers every step the POS device needs to perform: fetching the menu, placing an order, handling payment (cash or MoMo), updating order status, and viewing order history. The POS never needs to manage branch IDs or order sources — all of that is derived automatically from the pos_user_email.

authentication.note: All /api/* routes are public. No headers or tokens required. The POS device identifies itself and its branch using pos_user_email.

pos_credentials.description: Each POS device is issued a pos_user_email. When this email is sent with a checkout request, the server automatically: (1) looks up the branch linked to that email, (2) sets order_source to instore, (3) validates that the POS account and its branch are both active.

1_app_startup.description: When the POS app launches, load the menu and available tellers for the current branch.

steps.label: Load products
steps.note: Replace cape-coast with your branch slug. Returns only products available at this branch.

steps.label: Load toppings
steps.note: Returns only toppings available at this branch. The first topping on each item is free.

steps.label: Load tellers for the branch
steps.note: Returns all active tellers assigned to the branch linked to that POS email. Use this to populate a teller selector at the start of each shift.

2_place_order.description: Customer selects items. Teller inputs the order and phone number, then submits. The server validates everything, creates the order record, and (for MoMo/Hubtel) initiates the payment prompt on the customer's phone.

steps.label: Submit checkout
notes: pos_user_email is required. Do NOT send branchSlug when using POS mode.
phone must be a valid 10-digit Ghanaian number (0XXXXXXXXX). The server normalises it to 233XXXXXXXXX.
locationText for in-store orders should be 'In-store' or the counter number.
variantId is required if the product has variants, otherwise send null.
toppings array can be empty. The first topping per item is free automatically.
sugarLevel and spiceLevel are optional free-text strings (e.g. '50%', 'mild').

success_response_hubtel.message: Order created. Redirect customer to checkout URL or show QR code.

steps.success_response_cash_note: For cash payments, place the order normally using this endpoint (payment will default to hubtel), then immediately PATCH the order to set paymentMethod=cash and paymentStatus=paid (see Flow 3).

400_no_email.message: Either pos_user_email or branchSlug is required

400_branch_inactive.message: Branch linked to 'x@x.com' is currently inactive

400_product_not_available.message: Item 1: product 'X' is not available at this branch

400_out_of_stock.message: Item 1: product 'X' is out of stock

400_topping_unavailable.message: Item 1: topping 'X' is not available at this branch

400_invalid_phone.message: Invalid phone number — expected 10-digit Ghanaian number

3_cash_payment.description: Customer pays with cash. After collecting the money, immediately mark the order as paid via PATCH.

steps.label: Place order (same as Flow 2, Step 1)
steps.note: Submit the checkout request. Save the orderId from the response.

steps.label: Mark order paid by cash
notes: tellerId is the ID of the teller who processed the order (from the tellers list loaded at startup).
Set status to confirmed to move it into the kitchen queue.
paymentMethod must be one of: hubtel, cash, momo.

4_momo_payment.description: Customer pays via MoMo. The server sends a payment prompt to the customer's phone via Hubtel. The POS polls for the result.

steps.label: Place order (same as Flow 2, Step 1)
steps.note: The server automatically initiates a Hubtel MoMo prompt on the customer's phone. Save clientReference from the response.

steps.label: Poll payment status
steps.note: Poll every 5 seconds until paymentStatus is 'paid' or 'failed'. Timeout after 3 minutes.
notes: The Hubtel callback automatically sets paymentStatus=paid and status=confirmed when the customer approves the prompt.
Once status is confirmed, stop polling and show the confirmation screen.
If paymentStatus becomes 'failed', prompt the teller to retry or switch to cash.

steps.label: Attach teller to order after payment
steps.note: Optional but recommended: link the teller who handled this transaction for analytics.

5_update_order_status.description: Move the order through the kitchen workflow. Each status change is a PATCH to the same endpoint.

steps.label: Mark as ready (ready for pickup / handover)

6_view_orders.description: Fetch the order queue for the current branch. Use this to display active orders on the POS screen.

steps.label: Fetch all orders for this branch
steps.note: Returns the 50 most recent orders for the branch, newest first.

steps.label: Fetch only active orders (not completed or cancelled)
Fetch only unpaid orders (for cash recovery)
Fetch a single order status by clientReference

7_edit_order.description: All order edits go to the same PATCH endpoint. You can update any combination of fields in a single request.

patchable_fields.status: pending | confirmed | preparing | ready | completed | cancelled

examples.scenario: Add a note to an order

endpoint_summary.description: Fetch all active, in-stock products for a branch (with variants)
Fetch all active, in-stock toppings for a branch
Fetch tellers assigned to the POS device's branch
Place a new order. Returns orderId, orderNumber, clientReference, and totalGhs.
Poll payment and order status by clientReference
Fetch order queue for the branch with optional filters
Update order: status, paymentStatus, paymentMethod, tellerId, notes

pricing_rules.first_topping_free: The first topping on each order item is always free. priceAppliedPesewas will be 0 for the first topping and the full price for subsequent ones.
pricing_rules.variants: If a product has variants, the variant price overrides the product base price. Always send variantId when variants exist.
pricing_rules.total_calculation: Total = sum of (unitPesewas * quantity) + all topping priceAppliedPesewas across all items

phone_number_format.rule: Always send a valid 10-digit Ghanaian number starting with 0. The server normalises it.

common_errors.message: Either pos_user_email or branchSlug is required
common_errors.fix: Always include pos_user_email in every checkout request

common_errors.message: Provide either pos_user_email or branchSlug, not both
common_errors.fix: Remove branchSlug when using POS mode

common_errors.message: Item N: product 'X' is not available at this branch
common_errors.fix: Refresh your local menu cache — the product was removed from this branch

common_errors.message: Item N: product 'X' is out of stock
common_errors.fix: Remove the item and inform the customer. Refresh the menu.
Check that the pos_user_email is correct and exists in the system
Contact the admin to reactivate the POS account
